message:
fix(runtime): env-mutating tests use global lock to prevent races

Introduce a global ENV_LOCK mutex in test_utils.rs and an env_lock()
accessor so tests that call std::env::set_var/remove_var serialize
properly. Without this, concurrent test threads can see each other's
env mutations causing flaky failures.

builder.rs:
- Add with_clean_env() helper that acquires env_lock + cleans vars
- Wrap with_envs() in with_clean_env() so the lock is held during
  the entire set-env/run/unset-env lifecycle
- Remove standalone clean_env() calls from individual tests since
  with_envs() now handles it internally
- Wrap bare clean_env test in with_clean_env closure

env_config.rs:
- Apply same env_lock pattern to env_config tests

test_utils.rs:
- Add static ENV_LOCK: Mutex<()> for cross-test env serialization
- Add pub(crate) fn env_lock() returning MutexGuard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
